pi_items.verified_value: IFR Low-Altitude Enroute chart (FAA standard chart for IFR navigation below FL180/18,000 feet MSL)
pi_items.source: Code analysis of verifier.py lines 136-153 checks for 'ifr' + ('low' or 'enroute'); FAA AeroNav Products documentation confirms IFR Low-Altitude Enroute Charts display Victor airways, MEAs, VORs, and reporting points; charts updated every 56 days

privileged_info_summary: This task involves real-world aviation data: KLAX (Los Angeles International, 33.942791°N 118.410042°W) and KJFK (John F. Kennedy International, 40.641766°N 73.780968°W) are authentic commercial airports. The required routing uses real FAA IFR Low-Altitude Enroute Charts (updated every 56 days) that display Victor airways, Minimum Enroute Altitudes (MEAs), VOR navigation aids, and compulsory reporting points for instrument flight planning. The task requires 6 waypoints minimum (2 endpoints plus 4 intermediate waypoints including navaids, fixes, or airports) and operates with real Avare aviation GPS application data. The data is not synthetic but user-generated through interaction with the Avare app, which uses real FAA aviation databases and navigation data.
